questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The number of species below that have two lone pairs of electrons in their central atom is ___ (Round off to the Nearest integer) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi>BF</mi><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>ClF</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>AsF</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>BrF</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>XeF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>SF</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math>
questions.solution:
Two l.p. on central atom is =ClF3,XeF4

The valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory is a model used to predict 3-D molecular geometry based on the number of valence shell electron bond pairs among the atoms in a molecule or ion. This model assumes that electron pairs will arrange themselves to minimize repulsion effects from one another.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Consider, PF5,BrF5,PCl3,SF6,ICl4-,ClF3 and IF5

Amongst the above molecule(s) ion(s), the number of molecule(s)/ion(s) having sp3d2 hybridisation is
questions.solution:
Number of hybrid orbitals = Number of bond pair + number of lone pair on central atom.

To have sp3d2 hybridisation there should be total of 6 (bond pairs + lone pairs). The structure in such case will be octahedral or its derivative structures.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Amongst the following the number of oxide(s) which are paramagnetic in nature is

Na2O,KO2,NO2, N2O,ClO2,NO,SO2,Cl2O
questions.solution:
The magnetic properties of a substance can be determined by examining its electron configuration: If it has unpaired electrons, then the substance is paramagnetic and if all electrons are paired, the substance is then diamagnetic.

KO2 is potassium superoxide. There is one unpaired electron in anti-bonding molecular orbital of superoxide. Potassium superoxide is paramagnetic due to the presence of unpaired electron.

NO2,ClO2,NO are odd electron species. Hence, they are paramagnetic.

The other are monoxide and dioxide contain paired electrons, so they will show diamagnetic behaviour.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Among the following species <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> the number of species showing diamagnetism is
questions.solution:
The molecules with unpaired electrons are paramagnetic and molecules with all electrons paired are diamagnetic. In general molecules with an even number of electrons (except 10 and 16 electrons) are diamagnetic

N214e- diamagnetic

N2+13e- paramagnetic 

N2-15e-paramagnetic

N22-16e- paramagnetic

O216e-paramagnetic

O2+15e-paramagnetic

O2-17e-paramagnetic

O22-18e-diamagnetic

questions.q: In the cobalt-carbonyl complex : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><msub><mi>Co</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><mi>CO</mi></mfenced><mn>8</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math>, number of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Co</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>Co</mi></math> bonds is "<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>" and terminal <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> ligands is " <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math>". <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>=</mo></math>___
questions.solution: The number of Co-Co bonds are 1 and the number of Co-CO terminal bonds are 6. Hence, x+y=7.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The spin-only magnetic moment value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ion (in gaseous state) from the pairs <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Cr</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Cr</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Co</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Co</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> that has negative standard electrode potential, is_____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Negative value of electrode potential for M3+M2+ is for Cr+3Cr+2

ECr+3Cr+20=-0.41V

Cr+2 acts as a reducing agent readily

Electronic configuration of Cr+3 ion is

Ar4s03d3

Number of unpaired electrons,n=3

Magnetic moment,

μ=nn+2B.M   =33+2B.M

=15 B.M≃4 B.M

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
If CuH2O42+ absorbs a light of wavelength 600 nm for d-d transition, then the value of octahedral crystal field splitting energy for CuH2O62+ will be____×10-21 J [Nearest integer]

(Given : h=6.63×10-34Js and c=3.08×108 ms-1)
questions.solution:
Δt=hcλ=6.63×10-34×3.08×108600×10-9=340.34×10-21 J

Δ∘=94Δt=94×340.34×10-21=765.765×10-21≈766×1021 J

Note: According to official answer key provided by NTA answer is 745. But according to calculations answer is = 766.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In the ground state of atomic Fe(Z=26), the spin-only magnetic moment is ×10-1BM. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
[ Given :3=1.73,2=1.41]
questions.solution:
Fe→[Ar]4 s23 d6 

Number of unpaired e-=4

μ=4(4+2) B.M.

μ=24B.M

μ=4.89B.M

μ=48.9×10-1 B.M

Nearest integer value will be 49.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The spin-only magnetic moment value of the compound with strongest oxidizing ability among <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>MnF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>MnF</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>MnF</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is_____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> (nearest integer)
questions.solution:
The change from Mn3+ to Mn2+ results in the half-filled (d5 ) configuration which has extra stability.So, MnF3 has most oxidising tendency.

Hence, MnF3 has the strongest oxidizing ability as it undergoes reduction readily.

Spin only magnetic moment is calculated using the following formula

μ=nn+2B.M

μ=44+2=24=4.89=5 B.M

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Consider the following metal complexes :

CoNH33+

CoClNH352+

Co(CN)63-

CoNH35H2O3+

The spin-only magnetic moment value of the complex that absorbs light with shortest wavelength is B.M. (Nearest integer)
questions.solution:
The complex that absorbs minimum wave length of light has maximum splitting

Δ0∝1λΔ0=Crystal field stabilization energy

Here, CN- being strong field ligand ,It will have cause CFSE So,Co(CN)63- will absorb minimum wavelength.

The hybridization of Co in Co(CN)63- will be d2sp3

As, the complex has no unpaired electrons, spin only  magnetic moment will be zero and it is diamagnetic in nature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The oxidation state of manganese in the product obtained in a reaction of potassium permanganate and hydrogen peroxide in basic medium is
questions.solution:
This KMnO4 is a powerful oxidizing agent in neutral, acidic, and alkaline medium. The H2O2 is also an oxidizing agent but in the reaction of KMnO4 with the H2O2. The H2O2 loses its electron and acts as a reducing agent. In an acidic medium, theMnO4- ions involve the addition of 5 electrons and the two peroxide ions give out two electrons. In dilute alkaline medium or in neutral medium MnO4- ions involve the addition of 3 electrons. The reaction in basic medium is given below.

2KMnO4+3H2O2⟶basic medium2MnO2+4+3O2+2H2O+2KOH

The oxidation state of Mn in MnO2 is +4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Acidified potassium permanganate solution oxidises oxalic acid. The spin-only magnetic moment of the mangenese product formed from the above reaction is B.M.___(Nearest Integer)
questions.solution:
2KMnO4+5H2C2O4+3H2SO4→K2SO4+2MnSO4+10CO2+8H2O

In acidic medium, Mn7+ gets reduced to Mn2+ 

Mn (II) =Ar3d5

Number of unpaired electron in Mn2+=5

μ=nn+2 B.M

μ=55+2 B.M

μ=5.9 B.M

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An acidified manganate solution undergoes disproportionation reaction. The spin-only magnetic moment value of the product having manganese in higher oxidation state is____B.M. (Nearest integer)
questions.solution:
3MnO42-+4H+⟶2MnO4-+MnO2+2H2O

As we can see in this reaction oxidised product of reaction is MnO4- which has no unpaired electrons. So, magnetic moment will be zero.

Mn= no. of unpaired electrons is '0'

μ=0 B.M
